volumeTitle: Stichus. Trinummus. Truculentus. Tale of a Travelling Bag. Fragments
label: Truculentus (Section 4.2.715 - Section 4.3.784)

sections.label: Section 4.2.715
sections.translation: I meantime will stay here behind and watch at this door so long as he is thus transporting his presents home to you ; nor , in the meantime , will I admit any one from there to you who may cause you annoyance . Do you go on , just as you please . Are you not diddling these fellows ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.719
sections.original: Quis ístest , Astaphium , índica , qui périt ?
sections.translation: How now , Astaphium , tell me , who is this fellow that ’ s on the road to ruin ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.719b
sections.translation: Prithee , were you here ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.720
sections.translation: What — am I troublesome ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.720b
sections.translation: More now than you were ; for unless a person is of use to us , he is troublesome to us . But , prithee , do lend me your attention , that I may say what I want .

sections.label: Section 4.2.723
sections.original: Nám quid est ? núm mea réfert ?
sections.translation: Why , what is it ? Does it concern myself ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.723b
sections.translation: Not a rap . But what hauls he is making present of in-doors .

sections.label: Section 4.2.724b
sections.translation: How ? Some new lover ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.725
sections.translation: A fresh one , and a brimming treasure she has hit upon .

sections.label: Section 4.2.725b
sections.translation: Who is he ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.726
sections.original: Éloquar , sed tú taceto . nóstin tu hunc Strabácem ?
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you , but you be mum . Don ’ t you know this Strabax ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.726b
sections.translation: Why shouldn ’ t I ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.727
sections.original: Sólus summam núnc habet hic ápud nos , nunc is est fúndus nobis .
sections.translation: He alone rules the roast here at our house . He just now is a landed estate to us . With right good spirit is he wantonly wasting away his property .

sections.label: Section 4.2.728b
sections.translation: He ’ s on the road to ruin ; i ’ faith , I , too , have come to ruin .

sections.label: Section 4.2.730
sections.original: Stúltus es , qui facta infecta facere verbis postules .
sections.translation: You are a simpleton , to expect with words to make undone what is done .

sections.label: Section 4.2.731
sections.original: Thetis quoque etiam lamentando pausam fecit filio .
sections.translation: Even Thetis , too , in weeping , made lamentation for her son .

sections.label: Section 4.2.732
sections.original: Non ego nunc intro ad vos mittar ?
sections.translation: Can I not now be admitted in-doors to your house ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.732b
sections.translation: Why so rather than the Captain ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.733
sections.translation: Why , because I ’ ve given more .

sections.label: Section 4.2.733b
sections.original: Plus enim es íntro missus , quom dabas :
sections.translation: But you were admitted more , when you were giving more ; let those who give , in return for that which they give , enjoy our services .

sections.label: Section 4.2.735
sections.original: litteras didicisti : quando scis , sine alios discere .
sections.translation: You ’ ve learnt your letters ; since you know them yourself , let others learn them .

sections.label: Section 4.2.736
sections.original: Discant , dum mihi commentari liceat , ni oblitus siem .
sections.translation: Let them learn , so long as it is allowed me to con my lesson , that I may not forget what I have paid for .

sections.label: Section 4.2.737
sections.original: Quid erit interea magistrae , dum tu commentabere ?
sections.translation: In the meantime , while you , who are a master , shall be conning your lesson , she , as well , is desirous to con hers .

sections.label: Section 4.2.738b
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.738c
sections.translation: In receiving money ever and anon .

sections.label: Section 4.2.739
sections.original: Dedi equidem hodie : iussi ei quinque argenti deferri minas ,
sections.translation: For my own part , this very day I gave five minae of silver to be carried to her ,

sections.label: Section 4.2.740
sections.translation: besides one for provisions .

sections.label: Section 4.2.740b
sections.translation: I know that the same was brought ; with it we are now enjoying ourselves upon your liberality .

sections.label: Section 4.2.741b
sections.translation: For these enemies of mine here to be devouring my property ! By heavens , I ’ d rather that I were dead than submit to that !

sections.label: Section 4.2.746
sections.translation: You are a simpleton .

sections.label: Section 4.2.746b
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.746c
sections.translation: Because , I ’ troth , I ’ d rather

sections.label: Section 4.2.743
sections.original: mihi inimicos invidere , quam med inimicis meis ;
sections.translation: that my enemies should envy me , than I my enemies ; for to envy because it goes well with another , and goes badly with yourself , is wretchedness .

sections.label: Section 4.2.745
sections.original: qui invident egent ; illis quibus invidetur , i rem habent .
sections.translation: Those who are envious , are in want ; they who are envied , possess property .

sections.label: Section 4.2.747
sections.original: Non mei licet obsoni me participem fieri ?
sections.translation: May I not be a partaker of the provisions bought with the mina ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.748
sections.original: Si volebas participari , auferres dimidium domum .
sections.translation: If you wanted to be a partaker , you should have taken half home . For here an account of the receipts is entered just as at Acheron ;

sections.label: Section 4.2.750
sections.original: intro accipitur ; quando acceptumst , non potest ferri foras .
sections.translation: we take in-doors ; when it ’ s got by us , it can ’ t be carried out of doors . ( Turning on her heel .) Kindly farewell .

sections.label: Section 4.2.751b
sections.translation: Do stay .

sections.label: Section 4.2.751c
sections.translation: Let me go ! Leave off !

sections.label: Section 4.2.751d
sections.translation: Do let me go in .

sections.label: Section 4.2.751e
sections.translation: Yes , to your own house ,

sections.label: Section 4.2.752
sections.original: Immo istoc ad vos volo ire .
sections.translation: Aye , but here into your house .

sections.label: Section 4.2.752b
sections.translation: You cannot go .

sections.label: Section 4.2.753
sections.translation: I can , very well . Do let me try .

sections.label: Section 4.2.753b
sections.translation: No , wait here ; it ’ s sheer violence to try .

sections.label: Section 4.2.754
sections.translation: I ’ d say that you are here , if she wasn ’ t engaged .

sections.label: Section 4.2.755
sections.translation: Are you going to return or not ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.755b
sections.original: Vocat me quae in me potest plus quam potes .
sections.translation: I ’ d return , but a voice is calling me that has more influence with me than you have .

sections.label: Section 4.2.756
sections.translation: In one word

sections.label: Section 4.2.756b
sections.translation: I ’ ll say it .

sections.label: Section 4.2.756c
sections.translation: You ’ ll receive me ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.756d
sections.translation: You are telling a lie — be off . One word , you said ; but now three words have you uttered , and those untrue .

sections.label: Section 4.2.758
sections.original: Abiit intro , exclusit . egon ut haec mihi patiar fieri ?
sections.translation: She ’ s off , and she ’ s gone hence in-doors That I should endure these things to be done to me . By heavens , enticer , with my cries I ’ ll be exposing you to ridicule in the street ,

sections.label: Section 4.2.760
sections.original: quae adversum legem accepisti a plurimis pecuniam ;
sections.translation: you who , contrary to law , have received money from many a one . Upon my faith , I ’ ll forthwith cause your name to be before every magistrate [*] , and after that I ’ ll sue you for fourfold [*] , you sorceress , you kidnapper of children . By the powers , I ’ ll now disclose all your disgraceful deeds . Worthless creature that I am , who have lost everything I had ! I ’ m become desperate ,

sections.label: Section 4.2.765
sections.original: nec mi adeost tantillum pensi iam , quos capiam calceos .
sections.translation: and now I haven ’ t the slightest bit of concern what shoes I wear [*] . But why am I trying here ? What , suppose she were to order me to be let in ? I could swear in solemn form that I wouldn ’ t do it if she wished . It ’ s nonsense . If you thump a goad with your fists , your hands are hurt the most . It ’ s no good to be angry at a thing of nothing ; a creature that doesn ’ t value you a straw .

sections.label: Section 4.2.770
sections.original: sed quid hoc ést ? pro di immortales , Calliclem video senem ,
sections.translation: ( Starting .) But what ’ s this ? O immortal Gods , I see old Callicles , him who was my connexion by marriage [*] , bringing two female slaves in bonds , the one the hair-dresser of this Phronesium , the other his own servant-maid . I ’ m greatly alarmed ! inasmuch as one care has so recently taken possession of my heart , I ’ m afraid lest all my former misdeeds should be discovered .

sections.label: Section 4.3.775
sections.original: Egon tibi male dícam tibique aut male velim ? ut animus meust ,
sections.translation: Do I use ill language to you , or do I wish you so very ill ? According to my ideas , you have both pretty well experienced how mild and gentle a person I am . I interrogated you both , as you were lashed and hanging up by the arms ; I well remember it ; the way in which you quite confessed each point , I know . Here now , I wish in the same way to learn ; do you confess without a punishment .

sections.label: Section 4.3.780
sections.original: quamquam vos colubrino ingenio ambae estis , edico prius ,
sections.translation: Although you are both of you of the serpent nature , I tell you beforehand , you mustn ’ t be having double tongues , lest with your two tongues I should be putting you to death ; unless , perhaps , you wish to be taken to the men who go clink , clink [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.3.783
sections.original: Vis subigit verum fateri , ita lora laedunt bracchia .
sections.translation: Violence forces me to confess the truth ; the thongs do so gall my arms .

sections.label: Section 4.3.784
sections.original: At si verum mi eritis fassae , vinclis exsolvemini .
sections.translation: But , if you confess the truth to me , you shall be relieved from the chains .
